channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Meta’s New AI Asked for My Raw Health Data—and Gave Me Terrible Advice
item.description: Meta’s Muse Spark model offers to analyze users’ health data, including lab results. Beyond the obvious privacy risks, it’s not a capable stand-in for a real doctor.

item.title: Closing the Gap Between SCA Tools and Runtime Reality — Ashish Nadar
item.description: The alert came in on a Tuesday morning. A critical CVE. Severity score 9.8. Affecting one of the most widely used open-source libraries in the Node.js ecosystem. Our team had Snyk. We had Wiz. We...

item.title: Microsoft starts removing Copilot buttons from Windows 11 apps
item.description: Microsoft is starting to remove "unnecessary" Copilot buttons from its Windows 11 apps. In the latest version of the Notepad app for Windows Insiders, Microsoft has removed the Copilot button in...

item.title: I Built a Container Orchestrator in Rust Because Kubernetes Was Too Much and Coolify Wasn't Enough
item.description: There's a gap in the container orchestration world that nobody talks about. Docker Compose works for 1 server. Coolify and Dokploy give you a nice GUI but still cap at one node. Kubernetes handles...

item.title: How to Decide Which LMS Platforms Your Edtech Tool Should Support First in 2026
item.description: Most edtech teams make the same mistake early: they try to support every LMS at once. Canvas, Moodle, Blackboard, Brightspace, Schoology — the list grows until the integration work buries the...

item.title: Why Your App's Photos Look Weird: A Developer's Guide to Moiré Patterns
item.description: You've probably seen it before — strange rainbow-colored waves rippling across a photo of a computer screen, or weird grid-like artifacts in a scanned document. That's called a moiré pattern, and...

item.title: Infrastructure Design for Credit Risk Modeling
item.description: Common Problems Leading Institutions Face when Building Scorecards One version of the truth. Two people asking the same question, or repeating the same exercise should get the same answer. One way...

item.title: WHAT IS THIS RUBBER DUCK FOR?
item.description: This is a submission for the DEV April Fools Challenge What I Built The starting point was a brief moment in Harry Potter and the Chamber of Secrets, when Arthur Weasley asks Harry: "what exactly...

item.title: Project Glasswing and the Mythos Moment: A Critical Examination of AI's Cybersecurity Crossroads
item.description: On April 7, 2026, Anthropic announced Project Glasswing—a defensive cybersecurity initiative built around Claude Mythos Preview, a frontier AI model so capable at finding and exploiting...

item.title: Claude Code Skills vs MCP Servers — What to Use, How to Install, and the Best Ones in 2026
item.description: Claude Code has two main extension systems: skills (Markdown files) and MCP servers (protocol-based tools). Both make Claude Code more powerful, but they work differently and are best for...

item.title: Concurrency vs parallelism in Go: applied to Event Sourcing and CQRS
item.description: Rob Pike said in 2012: "Concurrency is about dealing with lots of things at once. Parallelism is about doing lots of things at once." Everyone nods at the conference. Nobody asks a question. And...

item.title: How Redis Caching Actually Works — A Visual Guide for Backend Developers
item.description: TL;DR: Redis caching sits between your application and database, storing frequently accessed data in memory for sub-millisecond reads. This guide walks through cache-aside, write-through, and...

item.title: Google Rolls Out DBSC in Chrome 146 to Block Session Theft on Windows
item.description: Google has made Device Bound Session Credentials (DBSC) generally available to all Windows users of its Chrome web browser, months after it began testing the security feature in open beta. The...

item.title: Backdoored Smart Slider 3 Pro Update Distributed via Compromised Nextend Servers
item.description: Unknown threat actors have hijacked the update system for the Smart Slider 3 Pro plugin for WordPress and Joomla to push a poisoned version containing a backdoor. The incident impacts Smart Slider...

item.title: OpenAI Backs Bill That Would Limit Liability for AI-Enabled Mass Deaths or Financial Disasters
item.description: The ChatGPT-maker testified in favor of an Illinois bill that would limit when AI labs can be held liable—even in cases where their products cause “critical harm.”

item.title: ChatGPT has a new $100 per month Pro subscription
item.description: OpenAI has announced a new version of its ChatGPT Pro subscription that costs $100 per month. The new Pro tier offers "5x more" usage of its Codex coding tool than the $20 per month Plus...

item.title: Here are the best Apple Watch deals available right now
item.description: In September, Apple launched its latest batch of smartwatches, including the Apple Watch Series 11, the SE 3, and the Ultra 3. Each has its own pros and cons, but the introduction of Apple’s...

item.title: Florida launches investigation into OpenAI
item.description: Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier is launching an investigation into OpenAI over public safety and national security risks, as reported earlier by Reuters. In a statement on Thursday,...

item.title: The EFF is quitting X
item.description: The digital privacy nonprofit Electronic Frontier Foundation will no longer be posting on X as of Thursday, largely due to a sharp decline in views on the platform over the past several years. In...

item.title: ChatGPT finally offers $100/month Pro plan
item.description: OpenAI announced on Thursday something that power users have been asking for: a $100/month plan. Previously, subscriptions jumped from $20 to $200 per month.

item.title: John Deere will pay farmers $99 million over right-to-repair lawsuit
item.description: John Deere has agreed to pay farmers $99 million to resolve a class action lawsuit that accused the agricultural giant of preventing farmers and mechanics from accessing the materials needed to...

item.title: Samsung’s Galaxy Watch 8 is easier to recommend now it starts at $260
item.description: Samsung’s Galaxy Watch 8 is one of the best Android smartwatches you can buy right now, especially if you’re already using a Samsung phone. Right now the 40mm, Bluetooth-enabled version’s on sale...

item.title: Florida AG announces investigation into OpenAI over shooting that allegedly involved ChatGPT
item.description: ChatGPT had reportedly been used to plan the attack that killed two and injured five at Florida State University last April. The family of one victim has said that they plan to sue OpenAI over the...

item.title: NASA’s Artemis II mission to fly around the far side of the Moon
item.description: Humans haven’t set foot on the Moon since NASA’s Apollo 17 mission in 1972. Now, the space agency is racing to get back to the lunar surface under the umbrella of its Artemis program — a nod to...

item.title: “Negative” views of Broadcom driving thousands of VMware migrations, rival says
item.description: Western Union exec says there were "challenges" working with Broadcom.

item.title: After data breach, $10B-valued startup Mercor is having a month
item.description: After falling victim to a hacker, Mercor is facing lawsuits and reportedly losing big-name customers.

item.title: Instagram now lets you edit your own comments
item.description: If you make a mistake in an Instagram comment, you can now finally fix it without having to delete the comment and make it again. On Thursday, Instagram announced that users can now edit their...

item.title: Meta AI app climbs to No. 5 on the App Store after Muse Spark launch
item.description: The app was ranking No. 57 on the App Store just before Meta AI's new model launched. Now it's No. 5 — and rising.

item.title: Is Anthropic limiting the release of Mythos to protect the internet — or Anthropic?
item.description: Anthropic said this week that it limited the release of its newest model, dubbed Mythos, because it is too capable of finding security exploits in software relied upon by users around the world....

item.title: Google and Intel deepen AI infrastructure partnership
item.description: The two tech giants are looking to co-develop custom chips, at a time when demand for CPUs is high due to a growing global shortage.
